findings.signature: function createCodeIndexMcpServer(): Server
findings.notes: Creates MCP Server with ListTools + CallTool handlers dispatching via codeGraphTools.dispatch(). IPC bridge + idle monitor + owner-lease lifecycle wired here. A new daemon service copies this pattern.

findings.signature: async function handleTool(name: string, args: Record<string, unknown>): Promise<MCPResponse | null>
findings.notes: Central dispatch: validates args via validateToolArgs(), routes to 8 handler functions. Returns null for unknown tools. Composable — wrap or extend for new tool surfaces.

findings.signature: async function dispatch(name: string, args: Record<string, unknown>): Promise<MCPResponse>
findings.notes: Thin wrapper over handleTool with unknown-tool error fallback. Called by index.ts CallToolRequestSchema handler.

findings.signature: const CODE_GRAPH_TOOL_SCHEMAS: ToolDefinition[]
findings.notes: Array of 8 MCP tool definitions (scan/query/status/context/classify/verify/apply/detect_changes). Published to ListTools. Reuse: import to register same tools on a different server surface.

findings.signature: function validateToolArgs(toolName: string, rawInput: Record<string, unknown>): Record<string, unknown>
findings.notes: Validates MCP input against published schema: additionalProperties:false, enum, minLength. Throws field-specific Error. Reuse in any MCP dispatch layer.

findings.signature: async function handleCodeGraphQuery(args: QueryArgs): Promise<{ content: Array<{ type: string; text: string }> }>
findings.notes: Handles outline/calls_from/calls_to/imports_from/imports_to/blast_radius. Calls ensureCodeGraphReady before read. Reuse: call directly for programmatic graph queries without MCP transport.

findings.signature: async function handleCodeGraphContext(args: ContextHandlerArgs): Promise<{ content: Array<{ type: string; text: string }> }>
findings.notes: LLM-oriented graph neighborhoods. Delegates to buildContext() from code-graph-context.ts. Blocked-read path with canonical readiness vocabulary.

findings.signature: async function handleCodeGraphScan(args: ScanArgs): Promise<{ content: Array<{ type: string; text: string }> }>
findings.notes: Full/incremental scan: indexFiles → persistIndexedFileResult → cross-file edge resolution → dangling prune. Scope-change and zero-node guards. Reuse: trigger programmatic reindex.

findings.signature: async function handleDetectChanges(args: DetectChangesArgs): Promise<MCPResponse>
findings.notes: Read-only preflight: maps unified-diff to structural symbols via line-range overlap. Blocks on stale graph. Reuse: integrate into CI/PR pipelines for impact analysis.

findings.signature: function getDb(): Database.Database
findings.notes: Returns singleton better-sqlite3 handle for code-graph.sqlite. All DB operations flow through this. Lazy-init via initDb(). Central seam for DB access.

findings.signature: function queryOutline(filePath: string): CodeNode[]
findings.notes: Returns all code_nodes for a file. Used by query handler outline op and detect_changes. Direct DB query — no MCP wrapper needed.

findings.signature: function queryEdgesFrom(symbolId: string, edgeType?: string): CodeEdgeTargetResult[]
findings.notes: Outbound edges with optional edge-type filter. Returns {edge, targetNode}. Core building block for call-graph and import queries.

findings.signature: function getStats(): { totalFiles, totalNodes, totalEdges, lastScanTimestamp, lastGitHead, dbFileSize, schemaVersion, nodesByKind, edgesByType, ... }
findings.notes: Comprehensive graph health stats. Used by status handler. Returns DB file size, schema version, node/edge breakdowns, parse health summary.

findings.signature: async function ensureCodeGraphReady(rootDir: string, options?: EnsureReadyOptions): Promise<ReadyResult>
findings.notes: Auto-triggers selective/full reindex before reads. Returns ReadyResult with freshness/action/reason. Every read handler calls this. Reuse: gate any code-graph-dependent operation.

findings.signature: function persistIndexedFileResult(result: ParseResult, opts?: { deferDanglingTargetPrune?: boolean }): void
findings.notes: Atomic per-file persistence: upsertFile → replaceNodes → replaceEdges. Transactional. Used by scan handler loop.

findings.signature: function buildReadinessBlock(ready: ReadyResult): CodeGraphReadinessBlock
findings.notes: Maps ReadyResult to CodeGraphReadinessBlock with canonicalReadiness + trustState. All 4 handlers emit this. Reuse: consistent readiness envelope for any new handler.

findings.signature: async function startIpcSocketServer(options: IpcSocketServerOptions): Promise<IpcSocketServerHandle>
findings.notes: Multi-client IPC bridge over Unix socket. Each client gets its own MCP Server instance. Shared by memory/code-index/skill-advisor daemons. Reuse for new daemon service.

findings.signature: function resolveIpcSocketPath(dbDir: string): string
findings.notes: Resolves daemon-ipc.sock path under the DB dir. Convention: <dbDir>/daemon-ipc.sock. Import for consistent socket path resolution.

findings.signature: const DATABASE_DIR: string
findings.notes: Resolved code-graph DB directory. Skill-local: .opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/database. Overridable via SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_DB_DIR. All DB paths derive from this.

findings.signature: function getEmbedderAdapter(provider: string, model: string, dimensionsOverride?: number): ExecutionRouterAdapter
findings.notes: Returns cached or new ExecutionRouterAdapter for provider:model. Handles ollama delegation and factory-backed providers (openai/voyage/hf-local). Credential cache invalidation on adapter rotation.

findings.signature: function resolveDimensions(provider: string, model: string, dimensions?: number): number
findings.notes: Resolves embedding dimensions: explicit override > startup profile match > default. Used by getEmbedderAdapter and reindex. Import for consistent dim resolution.

findings.signature: async function teardownEmbedderAfterSwap(provider: string, model: string): Promise<void>
findings.notes: Disposes cached direct adapter after active-embedder swap. Called by reindex completion. Ensures stale provider resources are released.

findings.signature: function getActiveEmbedder(db: Database.Database): ActiveEmbedder
findings.notes: Reads active embedder name/dim/provider from vec_metadata table. Returns DEFAULT_ACTIVE_EMBEDDER when unset. Used by reindex and reconcile.

findings.signature: function ensureVecTableForDim(db: Database.Database, dim: number): string
findings.notes: Creates vec_<dim> table if missing. Returns table name. Used by reindex before writing vectors. Idempotent.

findings.signature: function startReindex(options: StartReindexOptions, runtimeOptions?: ReindexRuntimeOptions): string
findings.notes: Queues embedder reindex job: reads memory_index rows, embeds via adapter, writes vectors to staging shard, atomic rename on completion. Returns job ID.

findings.signature: function cancelJob(jobId: string, db?: Database.Database): ReindexJob | null
findings.notes: Sets job status to 'cancelled'. Worker checks status before each batch and aborts cleanly. Staging shard is cleaned up.

findings.signature: async function generateDocumentEmbedding(text: string): Promise<Float32Array | null>
findings.notes: Generates document embedding via lazy singleton provider. Circuit breaker: returns null on consecutive failures. LRU cache. Rate limiting. Primary embedding entry point for indexing.

findings.signature: async function generateQueryEmbedding(query: string): Promise<Float32Array | null>
findings.notes: Query embedding with lower cache priority than documents. Circuit breaker. Use for search query vectorization.

findings.signature: function invalidateProviderSingleton(): void
findings.notes: Drops cached provider singleton so next getProvider() re-resolves. Call after active-embedder pointer changes (e.g. completed reindex). Prevents stale model/dim usage.

findings.signature: class BoundedMap<K, V> extends Map<K, V> { readonly maxSize: number }
findings.notes: LRU-evicting Map with maxSize cap. Access promotes entry to most-recent. Reuse for any bounded in-memory cache.

findings.signature: class TtlMap<K, V> { set(key, value, ttlMs?), get(key), pruneExpired() }
findings.notes: TTL + LRU bounded cache. BoundedMap-backed. Auto-prunes expired entries on access. Reuse for time-sensitive caches.

findings.signature: function rotateIfNeeded(auditPath: string, maxBytes: number, maxFiles: number, now?: Date): boolean
findings.notes: Rotates audit log when exceeding maxBytes, enforces maxFiles cap. Reuse for any log rotation need.
